title: wolftpm-tss: implement Connection for Linux /dev/tpm0 and swtpm
description:
Implement tpm2_rs_client::connection::Connection for wolfTPM's Linux and swtpm transports.

## What
WolfTpmLinuxDev and WolfTpmSwtpm both implement Connection::transact by:
1. Copying cmd bytes into rsp buffer (wolfTPM transport is in-place)
2. Calling TPM2_CTX::ioCb directly — the only raw byte I/O interface wolfTPM exposes
3. Parsing response length from rsp[2..6] (big-endian u32, TCG TPM2 response header)
4. Returning &rsp[..resp_len]

## Files
- wolftpm-tss/src/connection.rs

## Transact bridge pseudocode
rsp[..cmd.len()].copy_from_slice(cmd);
let rc = unsafe { (ctx.ioCb.unwrap())(ctx, rsp.as_ptr(), rsp.as_mut_ptr(), cmd.len() as u16, ctx.userCtx) };
Error::check(rc)?;
let resp_len = u32::from_be_bytes(rsp[2..6].try_into().unwrap()) as usize;
Ok(&mut rsp[..resp_len])

## Test
Smoke test: use tpm2_rs_client::get_capability or run_command(GetRandom { bytes_requested: 32 }) against swtpm.
Requires swtpm running locally — gate behind a feature flag or cfg(test) ignore annotation.
